content:
# User Story 1: Upload Bank Statement
## Description
As a user, I want to upload my bank statement to the Personal Finance App, so that I can analyze my financial transactions.
## Acceptance Criteria

### AC1: Successful Upload
- **Given** the user has a bank statement file in a supported format
- **When** the user uploads the file
- **Then** the system processes the file and extracts relevant transaction data
- **And** the user is notified of a successful upload

### AC2: Invalid File Format
- **Given** the user has a bank statement file in an unsupported format
- **When** the user attempts to upload the file
- **Then** the system notifies the user of an invalid file format
- **And** provides instructions on supported formats

### AC3: File Upload Error
- **Given** the user's file upload encounter an error
- **When** the upload fails
- **Then** the system notifies the user of the upload failure
- **And** provides guidance on troubleshooting steps

## Relevant docs
Feature spec: [Personal Finance App Specification](Link to spec)
Technical Notes: File processing will utilize a microservices architecture for scalability and maintainability.

---

# User Story 2: AI-Powered Transaction Categorization
## Description
As a user, I want the app to automatically categorize my transactions using AI, so that I can easily track my spending habits.
## Acceptance Criteria

### AC1: Categorization Accuracy
- **Given** the user has uploaded a bank statement with various transactions
- **When** the system applies AI-powered categorization
- **Then** transactions are categorized with a high degree of accuracy (at least 90%)
- **And** the user can review and correct categorization mistakes

### AC2: Learning from User Corrections
- **Given** the user corrects categorization mistakes
- **When** the system learns from these corrections
- **Then** the system improves its categorization accuracy over time
- **And** the user benefits from more accurate categorization in future uploads

### AC3: Categorization Options
- **Given** the user wants to adjust categorization settings
- **When** the user accesses categorization options
- **Then** the system provides customizable categorization settings
- **And** the user can apply these settings to future categorization tasks

## Relevant docs
Feature spec: [Personal Finance App Specification](Link to spec)
Technical Notes: AI categorization will leverage machine learning algorithms to improve accuracy based on user feedback.

---

# User Story 3: Financial Summary and Insights
## Description
As a user, I want the app to provide a clear and understandable financial summary, so that I can gain insights into my spending habits and financial situation.
## Acceptance Criteria

### AC1: Summary Generation
- **Given** the user has uploaded and categorized transactions
- **When** the system generates a financial summary
- **Then** the summary includes key financial metrics (e.g., income, expenses, savings)
- **And** the summary is presented in a user-friendly format

### AC2: Spending Patterns Analysis
- **Given** the user wants to analyze spending patterns
- **When** the system analyzes categorized transactions
- **Then** the system identifies trends and patterns in the user's spending
- **And** the user can view these insights to inform future financial decisions

### AC3: Customizable Summary
- **Given** the user wants to customize the financial summary
- **When** the user accesses summary settings
- **Then** the system provides options for customizing the summary (e.g., date range, categories)
- **And** the user can apply these settings to view a tailored financial summary

## Relevant docs
Feature spec: [Personal Finance App Specification](Link to spec)
Technical Notes: Financial summaries will be generated using data from the categorized transactions, leveraging data visualization techniques for clarity.

---

# User Story 4: Transaction Sharing
## Description
As a user, I want to share transactions with another user or share a specific set of accounts from all transactions, so that I can collaborate on financial management with others.
## Acceptance Criteria

### AC1: Transaction Sharing
- **Given** the user selects transactions to share
- **When** the user initiates transaction sharing
- **Then** the system shares the selected transactions with the specified user
- **And** the recipient can view the shared transactions

### AC2: Account Sharing
- **Given** the user selects specific accounts to share
- **When** the user initiates account sharing
- **Then** the system shares the selected accounts with the specified user
- **And** the recipient can view transactions from the shared accounts

### AC3: Sharing Permissions
- **Given** the user wants to manage sharing permissions
- **When** the user accesses sharing settings
- **Then** the system provides options for controlling what is shared and with whom
- **And** the user can adjust permissions as needed

## Relevant docs
Feature spec: [Personal Finance App Specification](Link to spec)
Technical Notes: Transaction sharing will utilize secure data transfer protocols to protect user financial information.

---

These user stories cover the primary workflows and features of the Personal Finance App, ensuring that the system meets the requirements outlined in the specification. Each story is designed to be independent, valuable, estimable, small, and testable, following the INVEST principles for effective agile development.
